description_long: This is a rounded ceramic vase with a narrow neck and a small, slightly flared mouth. The body is decorated with a subtle, light floral motif, likely a lotus, rendered in a soft, almost monochromatic palette of off-white and grayish-beige. The upper shoulder of the vase features a geometric border pattern, adding a refined accent to the overall design. The surface appears smooth and slightly glossy, suggesting a glazed finish typical of fine pottery. The vase's form is symmetrical and well-proportioned, with a gentle taper from the widest point of the body to the base and neck. This decorative piece would be well-suited for display in elegant urban interiors or public spaces.
description: A rounded, glazed ceramic vase with a narrow neck, featuring soft floral motifs and a geometric border in off-white and grayish-beige tones.
description_view_0: Front view shows the main floral motif prominently displayed on the rounded body, with the geometric border visible near the neck.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the continuation of the floral pattern, with more abstract leaf shapes and less central detail.
description_view_2: Opposite front view, again highlighting the main floral motif, nearly symmetrical to view 0.
description_view_3: Side view similar to view 1, showing the abstract continuation of the floral and leaf patterns.
